note: Public security & compliance posture. Each control's status is honest: implemented controls cite a numbered gate (mNN) or a wiki/ doc that proves them; partial/planned controls say what is missing. This file is the SINGLE SOURCE served by GET /v1/trust and the spine of wiki/trust-center.md — they must agree. Flag-gated OFF (TRUST_CENTER_ENABLED) by default.

controls.name: Tamper-evident tenant audit log
controls.detail: Hash-chained per-tenant audit entries; the chain is recomputable so any insertion/edit/deletion is detectable. Gate m104 (audit-chain) proves chain integrity end-to-end and that a tampered row breaks verification.

controls.name: GDPR hard-erase (right to erasure)
controls.detail: Per-tenant scoped hard delete of a subject's data with a verifiable erasure record (migration 048). Gate m105 (hard-erase) proves the data is gone and that another tenant's data is never touched.

controls.name: Data export / portability (GDPR Art. 20)
controls.detail: Engine-neutral JSON bundle of ONE tenant's data with a manifest (tables, row counts, sha256). Strictly tenant-scoped (own schema or WHERE tenant_id = caller). Gate m109 (tenant-export).

controls.name: SOC2-lite continuous evidence collector
controls.detail: Automated evidence collector seals signed snapshots of CI gate results, access posture, and change-management trail (migration 051). Gate m108 (soc2-evidence) proves collect/verify integrity. PARTIAL: this is internal evidence collection, NOT a formal SOC 2 Type II report — see formal-soc2-type2.

controls.name: Formal SOC 2 Type II report
controls.detail: Requires an independent external auditor over a multi-month observation window. Not yet engaged. The m108 evidence collector is the input that shortens that audit; it is not a substitute for it.

controls.name: Per-tenant data isolation (per-request RLS / owner-scope)
controls.detail: Isolation is enforced per REQUEST (app.current_tenant_id + owner predicate re-stamped on every request), not by pool state — which is why 10K tenants collapse onto 1 pool with byte-identical results to per-tenant pools. Gate m46 (share-pools-isolation) proves cross-engine isolation at density.

controls.name: Encryption in transit (TLS to engines + edge)
controls.detail: Under SECURITY_MODE=max, MSSQL verifies TLS by default and refuses insecure DSNs; mongo/redis insecure DSN params are rejected; CA-pin via DATA_PLANE_TLS_CA_FILE. PARTIAL: Postgres sslmode=require is accept-any OUTSIDE max (audit O4) — run max for multi-tenant. Edge TLS is operator-provided.

controls.name: Encryption at rest (per-tenant)
controls.detail: Disk/volume encryption is an operator/host responsibility today. Per-tenant encrypted-at-rest backups are a roadmap follow-up (B6 / audit solution #11). Not yet a Grobase-enforced control.

controls.name: Secrets management (Vault, dynamic DB creds)
controls.detail: Vault-backed secrets with credential_ref{provider:vault}; dynamic short-lived DB credentials reduce blast radius. PARTIAL: plaintext DATA_PLANE_MOUNTS are not yet forbidden outside max (audit O5) — enforce Vault refs in prod.

controls.name: ABAC policy decision point + owner-scoped writes
controls.detail: Attribute-based access with per-request owner-scoping on every engine; RLS as defense-in-depth on tenant tables. Org/RBAC scoping stays in the control plane (preserves the shared-pool density story).

controls.name: Network access control / IP allowlist
controls.detail: Per-tenant IP allowlist enforced at the control plane. Gate m106 (ip-allowlist) proves enforcement and flag-off parity. This is access control, NOT a full L7 WAF — see waf.

controls.name: Web Application Firewall (L7)
controls.detail: A managed L7 WAF (rule sets, rate-based rules, bot mitigation) is provided by the hosting edge in the managed-cloud offering; it is operator-supplied for self-host. Not yet a Grobase-enforced, gate-proven control.

controls.name: Phishing-resistant auth (WebAuthn passkeys)
controls.detail: Server-side WebAuthn registration + authentication ceremonies (migration 050), minting a GoTrue-shaped session. Gate m107 (passkeys) drives the full ceremony with a virtual authenticator and proves wrong-key/replay/cross-user rejection.

controls.name: Organizations / teams / members / RBAC
controls.detail: Control-plane org model with members, invites and role-based access. Gate m103 (orgs-rbac). Org scoping is control-plane only by design, preserving SHARE_POOLS multi-tenant density.

controls.name: Vulnerability disclosure (security.txt)
controls.detail: A security.txt + disclosure contact is part of the trust-center / status-page scaffold (D4.6). PARTIAL: the public status page + disclosure endpoint are on-demand infra not yet stood up; the policy and contact are defined.

controls.name: Uptime SLA + status page
controls.detail: Per-tier uptime targets (basic 99.5%, essential/pro 99.9%, max 99.95%) are TARGETS pending the C7 uptime probe. Until a probe writes durable availability samples, the SLA is not enforceable — stated honestly, not advertised as live.

controls.name: Supply-chain hardening (frozen lockfiles, SCA)
controls.detail: Frozen lockfiles everywhere; npm ci --ignore-scripts; pnpm minimum-release-age + onlyBuiltDependencies allowlist; cargo-audit on both Rust workspaces; SEMGREP + npm audit via make baas-security-scan.
